notes: BW11 Legendary Treasures, released 2013. Standard BW 10-card pack: 5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare-slot. Box = 36 packs. Sourced rates: EX (Pokemon-EX Full Art) at a generous 1:9 packs (Flipside Gaming "A Comprehensive Review of Rarity Part Two") -> 0.11 of rare slot. Secret rare (gold Reshiram/Zekrom, 2 cards) at the BW-era ~1:72 packs slot rate -> ~0.014. The gold full-art versions themselves are far rarer (reported 1 per 2 cases) but the secret-rare slot occupancy maps to the era ~1:72. Ultra-rare bucket here = the 5 Radiant Collection Full Arts (Rare Ultra); LT was famously generous on ultra-rares ("insane amount of ultra rares in a box"), mapped to ~1:18 (0.055), roughly double the standard BW 1:36 UR rate. Box-break reports cited ~18-21 holo rares per box of 36 -> rare-holo ~0.54 of rare slot; non-holo rare is the remainder. Reverse-holo slot lands on common/uncommon/rare/rare-holo pool (no EX in reverse slot for this era). confidence=medium: EX 1:9 and secret 1:72 are published era/set figures, but holo-per-box and UR generosity come from community box-break samples rather than a large per-set study. Vintage sealed box carries a heavy collector premium so EV/booster will sit well below box price - expected. Distribution keys are a subset of the set's available buckets {common, uncommon, rare, rare-holo, ex, secret-rare, ultra-rare}; each slot sums to 1.0.
